title: Discover with us the Tassili N'Ajjer National Park and its surrounding tourist sites
article:
The magic of the Algerian desert cannot be described in words, and the Tassili N'Ajjer Enclosure (classified as a World Heritage Site by UNESCO) is the largest open-air natural and art museum in the world. This area combines wondrous rock formations that resemble another planet, hidden green oases and wall paintings dating back thousands of years. ​Since you are planning a tourism program, I have collected for you the details of the most important sites I mentioned, and designed for you a balanced trip plan that combines adventure, culture, and relaxation. ​Guide to Notable Tourist Sites

​1. Tadrart Rouge Location and importance: It is located in the far south-east of the city of Djanet, and is famous for its sand, which ranges in color between yellow, red and dark orange. What do you see there? There you will see a stunning contrast between high sand dunes and black rocks eroded by the wind, in addition to huge natural arches and unique rock paintings (such as drawings of elephants and giraffes that prove that the desert was a green oasis in ancient times).

​2. Tikobaouine Location and Importance: Located north of the city of Djanet, it is a “city of rocks” or an amazing rock maze. What do you see there? The most prominent landmark here is the Tecopawin Natural Arch (a suspension rock bridge connecting two mountains), which is one of the most popular spots for sunset photos. The area also includes ancient archaeological cemeteries and breathtaking narrow sand paths.

​3. Iherir Oasis and Gorge (Iherir) Location and importance: A lush oasis classified as a wetland area of ​​international importance (Ramsar), located in the heart of the rocky plateau. What do you see there? It is a true paradise in the middle of the barren desert; Where you find emerald water pools (Gueltas) surrounded by palm trees and reeds, and springs flowing all year round. The area is inhabited by the local Toareg community, and there you can see traditional mud houses and visit the nearby “Deder” area to see the famous “Sleeping Deer” carving.

​4. Essendilène Valley Location and importance: A narrow and majestic rocky gorge, ending in a hidden pool. What do you see there? Walking inside this valley is a very enjoyable experience, as the high rock walls protect you from the sun’s rays, and oleander plants and acacia trees are spread throughout it. At the end of the strait, you will reach the “Galta Esendelen” (a pool of cold water surrounded by rocks on all sides).
